summary:
The patient requires an evaluation involving both serum prolactin level testing along with additional assessment such as measuring Thyroid-Stimulating Hormone due to potential underlying causes like hypothyroidism affecting prolactin production; thus providing more accurate diagnosis by considering broader physiological factors influencing hormonal balance related symptoms presented here.
The recommended investigations include not only serum prolactin test but also checking TSH because it helps identify conditions causing secondary elevation of prolactin associated with hypothalamic-pituitary disorders impacting reproductive health functions indicated clinically via signs/symptoms described above. To ensure no other contributing factor exists regarding abnormal lactation-menses pattern observed currently necessitating thorough endocrine panel review incorporating these two essential markers
